details: Semantic model was renamed to OEMInsightBI_v2 (spec corrected at lines 82/726; live report binds to ../OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel; old model archived under fabric/archive/), but the archived name semantic_model_oeminsightbi still appears as the active/current model across ~10 non-archive, non-audit surfaces: README.md, project_definition.md, docs/README.md, docs/portfolio/PORTFOLIO_ASSETS_README.md, docs/architecture/fabric-artifacts-inventory.md (listed 'Active'), task files 002/003/004/009/013/014/015, commands sync-from-fabric/sync-to-fabric/run-full-pipeline, and support/documents (dax_measures.md, architecture/semantic_model.md, fabric_workspace.md, standards/naming_standards.md). Flagged by the 2026-05-17 coherence audit (path-drift lens) but the cross-surface sweep was never completed. NOTE: some occurrences are legitimate historical references that must NOT be changed (the 2026-05-17 audit files, feedback/archive.md, and the spec's 'old name archived' notes) -- distinguishing requires per-file judgment. Candidate for an /audit-coherence sweep; out of scope for task-010 (whose only doc occurrence, line 149, was already fixed).
resolution: Cross-surface sweep completed via task-021 (subtasks 1a-1d). Global grep confirms semantic_model_oeminsightbi no longer appears as an active reference outside fabric/archive/, designated naming examples, and task-file provenance. Also completed the sibling sweeps for clean_columnsAndHeaders.Notebook (re-opened FB-001) and report.Report. Regression guard added: tests/test_doc_artifact_names.py.

details: docs/portfolio/PORTFOLIO_ASSETS_README.md L55/L287 reference _Measures.tmdl, which exists only under fabric/archive/semantic_model_oeminsightbi.SemanticModel/. The canonical OEMInsightBI_v2 model reorganized measures into per-table .tmdl files, so _Measures.tmdl has no v2 equivalent. task-021_1a corrected the directory stem (rename sweep) but the leaf filename remains a broken reference -- separate content-drift, out of scope for the rename. Fix: point the portfolio README at the v2 model's actual measure file(s) or describe measures generically.

error_handling_strategy.md (L234/310/347/424/1071) and incremental_load_strategy.md (L540/543) use 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' as a pipeline ACTIVITY / config identifier. Ground-truth from fabric/orchestrator_pipeline_bronze_to_gold.DataPipeline/pipeline-content.json: the real cleaning activity is named 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' and references notebooks by notebookId (GUID), not by name -- there is NO 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' activity. So these design-doc identifiers are stale illustrative examples that never matched the real pipeline activity. task-021_1d PRESERVED them (per the task's preserve-activity-name instruction, whose premise turned out to be wrong) rather than guessing the correct identifier. Decision needed: rename to 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning' (display name has spaces -> awkward in code examples), pick a code-identifier form, or leave as generic illustrative examples. Out of scope for the rename sweep.

Audit C-02 (spec names the EU supply-shares bronze table 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares' in Data Architecture/Transformations L320/356/402 but 'bronze_EUSupplyShares' in Orchestration L626) was grounded during /iterate and found to mirror a REAL implementation split, not a spec typo. The pipeline copy activity 'bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares' writes sink table 'bronze_EUSupplyShares' (pipeline-content.json:42), but bronze-to-silver.Notebook (L135) and data_quality_checks.Notebook (4x) READ 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares'. A whole-repo grep found NO writer/creator for 'bronze_GlobalSupplyShares' -- only the pipeline writing 'bronze_EUSupplyShares'. The semantic model PBI_RemovedChildren annotation lists BOTH names (a rename happened, one side not updated). LIKELY LINEAGE GAP: the orchestrated pipeline may not refresh the table silver consumes. PENDING: Erik to check oem_lh -- is bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (a) a manual rename of bronze_EUSupplyShares [then fix pipeline sink via /work], (b) a distinct intentional dataset [document both in spec], or (c) loaded externally [document load path, flag sink legacy]. Once confirmed: code fix via /work if needed, then reconcile spec via /iterate (standardize name or document both). C-03 + C-04 were resolved (spec edited 2026-06-22); C-02 deferred pending Fabric verification.
diagnostic_2026_06_22: Fabric check (Erik): BOTH tables exist in oem_lh, identical schema [Material,Stage,Country,Share,t], but FULLY DISJOINT data and different sizes. bronze_EUSupplyShares = 907 rows, lastModified 2026-01-19 (pipeline sink; read by nothing -> ORPHAN). bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 56342 rows, lastModified 2025-08-25 (read by bronze-to-silver + DQ checks -> the actual silver/gold input, but STALE ~10mo and NOT refreshed by the pipeline). => distinct datasets + crossed lineage: fact_supply_share gold is built on Aug-2025 data; the pipeline's bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares produces an orphan. PENDING design decision from Erik: is bronze_GlobalSupplyShares meant to be pipeline-refreshed (=> bug, repoint copy activity to Global + retire EU) or an intentional static/external dataset (=> document + retire EU orphan)? Then /work fix + /iterate spec doc.
diagnostic_2_2026_06_22: Confirmed via the source GitHub repo (Erik's, public). The pipeline's HTTP copy activity (connection GUID 97124587-4fe9-43ee-bd6c-6a0fe3bc5664, desc 'Copy EU CRM github repo csv file') wires ONLY fact_EUSupplyShares&t.csv -> bronze_EUSupplyShares (907 rows = CSV 907, MATCHES). The broader fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv (2561 rows, the table the project actually wants since the project scope is global) is NOT wired into the pipeline. CRITICAL: bronze_GlobalSupplyShares = 56342 rows = EXACTLY 22x the 2561-row source CSV -> strongly suggests a duplicated/cross-joined load (or a different source); the bronze table does not match its documented source and feeds silver -> fact_supply_share. RESOLUTION DIRECTION (Erik clarified project is broader than the EU-focused report): wire a copy activity for fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv -> bronze_GlobalSupplyShares (mirror the EU copy activity) so the pipeline produces the correct 2561-row global table; retire/keep EU as secondary. PENDING: Erik to (1) confirm the 22x duplication via distinct-count query, (2) then a /work task adds the Global copy activity + verify gold numbers, (3) /iterate spec note.

details: incremental_load_strategy.md prescribes natural-key MERGE + dedupe (silver, L223-266) and MERGE + optional aggregation (gold, L277-344); task-024 now uses transaction-grain date-partition delete-insert on BOTH layers per Erik's binding 2026-07-14 decision (keep transaction grain). The strategy doc must be reconciled to describe delete-insert + the task-029 watermark dependency. Covered by task-033 (documentation drift sweep).
source_anchor: incremental_load_strategy.md § Silver Layer Strategy (223-266) and § Gold Layer Strategy (277-344)

details: DQ framework's silver<->gold spend reconciliation (Check 8) reconciles EXACTLY only under uniform base units: gold computes spend_eur = quantity_base * unitprice_eur where quantity_base is kg-normalized (silver-to-gold2 L1216-1221) but unitprice_eur is NOT converted, so silver SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur) != gold SUM(spend_eur) for any non-kg unit. task-026 therefore classified reconciliation ADVISORY (blocking it would false-fail); the fan-out it used to guard is instead caught BLOCKING by the new lookup/grain uniqueness checks. Root cause (unit normalization / unmapped units -> kg) is task-030's scope; once units are confirmed all-kg or unitprice is converted, reconciliation can be promoted to blocking.

WGI bronze table name drift: docs (schemas/bronze_tables.md, architecture/medallion_architecture.md, architecture/orchestration.md, architecture/data_sources.md, fabric_workspace.md) document 'bronze_WB_ESGCSV' + 'bronze_WB_ESGSeries', but NO live artifact produces them -- WGI_file2table.Dataflow produces a single table 'bronze_WGI' (Excel-sourced; columns Country Name, Country Code, Series Name, Percentile Rank 2023) which bronze-to-silver consumes and the semantic model references. The stale doc name MISLED task-026's WGI checks (caused a verify FAIL). Reconcile the docs to 'bronze_WGI'.
